assertions.title: spikes login Command

steps.expected: Authentication flow completes and stores token
steps.observed: Command reports success and stores token
whoami fails with JSON parse error

assertions.issues: whoami does not work immediately after login because /me response is not parseable as JSON by the CLI.

assertions.title: spikes whoami Command

steps.action: Run whoami with a stored non-empty token
steps.observed: Command fails with JSON parse error

evidence.whoamiOutput: Error: JSON error: expected value at line 1 column 1

assertions.issues: whoami expects JSON from https://spikes.sh/me but current response is not parseable as expected UserInfo JSON.

steps.action: Run `spikes init --json` in a directory with existing .gitignore
Run `spikes init --json` in a directory without .gitignore

frictions.description: user-testing-flow-validator subagent repeatedly failed with permission-safety restrictions when executing CLI auth checks.
frictions.resolution: Executed CLI checks directly in this validator session with isolated HOME and captured evidence manually.

summary: Tested 6 CLI assertions: 4 passed (VAL-AUTH-012/013/015/017), 2 failed (VAL-AUTH-014/016). Login stores token correctly, env override precedence works, logout and init behaviors are correct; whoami does not return identity as specified.
